bullets.summary: NVIDIA reported Q3 FY2026 results on November 19, 2025: record revenue of $57.0 billion (+62% YoY), data center revenue $51.2 billion (+66% YoY), driven by faster-than-expected Blackwell GPU ramp. Q4 guidance set at $37.5B (±2%), ahead of consensus. These results represent the most recent earnings print as of January 31, 2026.

The DeepSeek-R1 release on January 22, 2025 caused NVDA to drop ~17% in a single session on fears that lower-cost model training would cut AI chip demand. By January 31, 2026 — one year on — NVIDIA and AI infrastructure spending had fully recovered and accelerated, with hyperscaler capex still expanding. The shock is now widely treated as a stress-test that the AI compute narrative passed.

Microsoft reported Q2 FY2026 earnings on January 28, 2026: revenue $81.3B (+17% YoY), Azure grew 39% YoY, Intelligent Cloud segment $32.9B (+29% YoY). Copilot now has 15 million commercial seats. Q3 Azure guidance set at 37-38% constant-currency growth.

OpenAI's $250 billion multi-year cloud commitment to Microsoft, booked in the quarter, boosted reported net income by $7.6 billion. This deepens MSFT's position as the infrastructure backbone for the leading frontier AI lab.

Alphabet's Q4 2025 earnings were scheduled for February 4, 2026 — outside the January 31 cutoff. Within the window, the most material event was Google Quantum AI opening Willow processor access proposals through May 2026, and the December 2024 Willow chip's quantum error-correction below the threshold milestone continuing to generate research momentum entering January 2026.

Alphabet signed a classified AI contract with the US Department of Defense, per Yahoo Finance reporting available in January 2026. This marks deepening of GOOGL's government AI exposure alongside its Cloud and DeepMind research pipelines.

On January 21, 2026, Rocket Lab disclosed that the Stage 1 propellant tank for its Neutron medium-lift rocket ruptured during hydrostatic qualification testing. No damage to structures or facilities; a replacement tank is in production. The failure pushed Neutron's first launch target from Q1 2026 to at least Q4 2026, delaying the company's entry into the medium-lift market.

On January 26, 2026, Rocket Lab confirmed the arrival of its 'Hungry Hippo' captive fairing at the Virginia launch site after clearing qualification testing — a positive milestone in Neutron's hardware maturation even as the tank setback pushes the full launch schedule. Separately, the company closed 2025 with a record 21 Electron launches at 100% mission success.

January 2026 saw multiple high-profile humanoid robot deployments: Boston Dynamics' Atlas debuted on 60 Minutes performing real factory work at a Hyundai plant in Georgia, and DeepMind announced a partnership to integrate Gemini Robotics foundation models into Atlas. Microsoft unveiled a robotics model (Rho-alpha) based on its Phi series for vision-language-action tasks.

Robotics investing reached an all-time high entering 2026, with public robotics companies outperforming the broader market and 10 new robotics IPOs in the prior two years per The Robot Report's 2026 State of Robotics Industry report. Amazon acquired Rightbot for truck-unloading automation; Wing Aviation expanded Walmart drone delivery to 150 additional stores.

In January 2026, ARK Invest executed a strategic portfolio shift within ARKG, materially increasing exposure to gene editing: ARK collectively purchased over 195,000 shares of Beam Therapeutics (base editing), raising its position value to approximately $5.4 billion. Simultaneously ARKG trimmed Ionis Pharmaceuticals, Natera, and Guardant Health.

The J.P. Morgan Healthcare Conference in San Francisco in January 2026 struck a cautiously optimistic tone for biotech: M&A activity and stock performance turned positive in H2 2025, drug-pricing policy from the Trump administration proved manageable for drugmakers, and interest rate declines improved the funding environment for loss-making biotech names that populate ARKG.

BTQ Technologies was added to the QTUM index (BlueStar Machine Learning and Quantum Computing Index) effective December 15, 2025, following the semi-annual reconstitution. BTQ's inclusion reflects growing institutional visibility for post-quantum cryptography names within the QTUM basket.

GQI's 2026 predictions published in January project that quantum hardware consolidation will accelerate as fundraising becomes harder and near-term market promises fall short of current valuations. First gate-based production-grade applications are expected in 2026 via error suppression advances, but logical qubits are forecast to deliver headline news with limited immediate commercial utility.

In December 2025, the US DOE selected Tennessee Valley Authority and Holtec to each receive $400 million in federal cost-shared funding to support early advanced light-water SMR deployments — TVA's Clinch River BWRX-300 and Holtec's Pioneer SMR-300 at the Palisades site. This is the most direct federal subsidy flow reaching NUKZ's core holdings as of January 31, 2026.

Trump's Day One executive orders (January 20, 2025) and subsequent May 2025 orders targeting quadrupled US nuclear capacity by 2050, NRC reform, and pilot reactor criticality by July 4, 2026 remain the policy backdrop. World Nuclear News reported US companies welcoming these orders and NextEra Energy signaling up to 6 GW of SMR additions at existing sites primarily to serve data-center demand.

As of late 2025, the Bloomberg US Aggregate Bond Index (which AGG tracks) carried a yield-to-worst of approximately 4.3% with a six-year average duration. The base-case 2026 consensus called for two to three Fed rate cuts, with the 10-year Treasury yield expected to range between 3.75% and 4.25%. Rates holding near the top of that range keeps AGG's total return modest.

The Fed cut rates by 25 bps at its final 2025 meeting to a 3.50-3.75% target range. The 2026 consensus calls for two to three additional cuts, but the pace depends on inflation stickiness. BIL (1-3 month T-bills) continues to yield near the upper bound of the Fed funds range — still competitive versus cash alternatives while rate cuts remain gradual.

IAU (iShares Gold Trust) hit an all-time closing high of $101.57 on January 29, 2026, capping a trailing twelve-month gain exceeding 80%. Drivers cited include central bank buying (projected ~585 tonnes/quarter), declining real yields from Fed easing, geopolitical escalations, and persistent fiscal deficits globally.

VIG (Vanguard Dividend Appreciation ETF) returned +1.9% in January 2026, modestly ahead of the Large Blend category average of +1.7%. The fund's top holdings include Microsoft and Eli Lilly; over 28% of the portfolio sits in technology stocks, giving it partial AI-cycle exposure within a dividend-growth wrapper.

AI.rationale: Real, accelerating revenue (MSFT Azure +39% YoY, NVDA data center +66% YoY) and massive committed capex from hyperscalers confirm that adoption is compounding into actual financial results. The DeepSeek scare a year prior proved to be a speed bump, not a cycle turn — spending accelerated through 2025 and into 2026. Enthusiasm is high but the financial delivery is keeping pace with the narrative, distinguishing this from 'peak' framing.

robotics.rationale: Humanoid deployments are beginning (Atlas on factory floors, Microsoft entering with a robotics model), investment is at all-time highs, and IPO activity is picking up — but actual revenue from humanoid robots remains nascent. The wave is transitioning from research to early pilots, which maps to late buildup rather than surge.

rockets_spacecraft.rationale: Rocket Lab achieved 21 Electron launches with 100% mission success in 2025, demonstrating operational scale. The Neutron tank failure introduces execution risk and a timeline slip, but the underlying contract pipeline (SDA missile-warning, Pentagon bids) and the Geost acquisition show the company expanding into higher-value national-security missions. The small-launch market is generating real recurring revenue.

engineered_biology.rationale: The JP Morgan Healthcare Conference struck a cautiously optimistic tone — sector is recovering from a multi-year funding drought, M&A is picking up, and ARK is leaning into early-stage gene editing names. But ARKG itself remains well below its 2021 peak and the portfolio is populated with pre-commercial or early-commercial companies. This is consistent with a late-buildup or early-surge stage, classified here as buildup given the fragility of the funding rebound.

quantum.rationale: GQI's January 2026 outlook flags hardware consolidation ahead and acknowledges that logical qubits will generate headlines but limited near-term commercial value. The Willow chip breakthrough and BTQ's index inclusion signal research momentum but not yet commercial revenue. Classic early-buildup: technically advancing, underpowered commercially.

nuclear_fusion.rationale: NUKZ's primary exposure is fission SMRs, not fusion — the fund holds Cameco, GE Vernova, Constellation Energy, and Talen Energy. The $400M DOE funding for TVA and Holtec (December 2025) is a meaningful policy tailwind but SMR deployments remain early 2030s. Strong policy support combined with distant commercial timelines is a textbook buildup signal.

general_markets.rationale: AGG, BIL, IAU, and VIG reflect macro conditions (moderately high rates, strong gold, steady dividend-growth equities) rather than a specific technology wave. No wave stage assigned.

thematic_concerns.concern: Neutron tank rupture on January 21, 2026 pushes first launch from Q1 to at least Q4 2026. This is a nine-month slip that delays Rocket Lab's entry into the medium-lift market and its ability to compete for larger Pentagon payloads. Execution risk is elevated relative to the growth thesis for the 2026 year.
thematic_concerns.evidence: Rocket Lab Neutron Test Update (January 21, 2026), SpaceNews — https://spacenews.com/rocket-lab-delays-neutron-debut-to-late-2026/

thematic_concerns.concern: The biotech recovery is fragile: ongoing regulatory uncertainty from RFK Jr.'s FDA posture and competition from Chinese biotech are cited by BioPharma Dive as the two live risks that could short-circuit the sector's resurgence. ARKG's concentration in pre-commercial genomics names amplifies downside if funding conditions tighten again.
